Details: Comparison of two Informatica XML mappings for MEMBERSDATA table processing. Basic_Mapping provides a minimal source definition with 13 fields from Snowflake database, while Complete_DQ_Mapping delivers a comprehensive data quality solution with transformations, validation rules, and dual output targets. The outputs serve different purposes - one as a foundation and the other as a complete implementation.

Both outputs address MEMBERSDATA table processing but with vastly different scopes. Basic_Mapping focuses solely on source definition, while Complete_DQ_Mapping implements comprehensive data quality validation with 15 DQ rules, routing logic, and separate valid/rejected outputs. The semantic intent differs significantly - basic data access versus complete data quality processing.

Structural approaches are fundamentally different. Basic_Mapping uses simple MAPPING structure with only SOURCE definition (lines 4-14), while Complete_DQ_Mapping employs complex POWERMART/REPOSITORY/FOLDER hierarchy with multiple transformation types: Expression (lines 18-49), Aggregator (lines 52-56), Router (lines 59-62), and dual targets (lines 65-95). Flow patterns are incompatible.

XML structure is well-formed with proper opening/closing tags. Source definition includes all required attributes. Minor issue: MEMBER_ID datatype inconsistency - defined as 'decimal' in XML but should align with typical integer usage for member IDs. All field definitions are syntactically correct.

XML structure is well-formed with proper hierarchy. Issues identified: Line 13 MEMBER_ID datatype mismatch (integer vs source decimal), Line 55 potential logic error in DQ_MEMBER_ID_UNIQUE expression (should use aggregated count), Lines 96-110 rejected target includes DQ flags but mapping flow may not populate them correctly.

column7: Both outputs demonstrate good XML syntax and structure. Complete_DQ_Mapping shows more complexity with minor logical inconsistencies in DQ rule implementation, while Basic_Mapping has simpler structure with one datatype alignment issue.
